description: Unsolved problems in computational complexity, algorithms, and computability. [length-matched]

facts.context: NP-intermediate problems (in NP but neither in P nor NP-complete) exist if:
facts.truth: P ≠ NP (Ladner's theorem); candidates include factoring, graph isomorphism
distractors: P = NP (Ladner's theorem); candidates include factoring, graph isomorphism
P ≠ NP (Ladner's theorem); candidates include graph isomorphism, traveling salesman problem
P = NP (Ladner's theorem); candidates include graph isomorphism, traveling salesman problem

facts.context: Graph isomorphism problem's complexity status is:
facts.truth: in quasipolynomial time (Babai 2015) but not known to be in P or NP-complete
distractors: in polynomial time but not known to be in NP-complete
in exponential time but not known to be in P
in NP-complete but not known to be in P

facts.context: The Unique Games Conjecture (Khot 2002) states that:
facts.truth: approximating unique label cover is NP-hard; implies many tight inapproximability results
distractors: approximating unique label cover is in P; implies many existing inapproximability results fail
approximating unique label cover is NP-hard; but has no implications for inapproximability
approximating unique label cover is coNP-hard; implies many tight approximability results

facts.context: The relationship BPP =? P concerns whether:
facts.truth: randomized polynomial time equals deterministic polynomial time (widely believed true, open)
distractors: randomized polynomial time strictly contains deterministic polynomial time (proven by Adleman)
randomized polynomial time is incomparable with deterministic polynomial time (independent of ZFC)
randomized polynomial time equals nondeterministic polynomial time (widely believed false, open)

facts.context: Whether NP = coNP is unknown. If NP ≠ coNP, then:
facts.truth: P ≠ NP (contrapositive: P = NP implies NP = coNP)
distractors: P = NP (contrapositive: P ≠ NP implies NP ≠ coNP)
P = NP (contrapositive: P ≠ NP implies NP = coNP)
P ≠ NP (contrapositive: P = NP implies NP ≠ coNP)

facts.context: The L vs NL problem asks whether:
facts.truth: nondeterministic log-space equals deterministic log-space (open; NL ⊆ P known)
distractors: The L vs NL problem asks whether non-deterministic log-space is equal to deterministic space complexity (open; L ⊆ P known)
The L vs NL problem asks whether non-deterministic log-space is equal to deterministic time complexity (open; NL ⊆ NP known)
The L vs NL problem asks whether non-deterministic log-space is equal to deterministic polynomial-time (open; NL ⊆ P known)

facts.context: The optimal exponent ω for matrix multiplication satisfies:
facts.truth: 2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.373 (conjectured ω = 2, current best upper bound by Williams et al.)
distractors: 2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.373 (conjectured ω = 3, current best upper bound by Williams et al.)
2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.373 (conjectured ω = 1, current best upper bound by Williams et al.)
2 ≤ ω ≤ 2.373 (conjectured ω = 1.5, current best upper bound by Williams et al.)

facts.context: The information-theoretic lower bound for comparison sorting n elements is:
facts.truth: Ω(n log n) comparisons, matched by mergesort and heapsort
distractors: Ω(n²) comparisons, matched by bubblesort and insertion sort
Ω(n) comparisons, matched by counting sort and radix sort
Ω(log n) comparisons, matched by binary search on sorted input

facts.context: The 3SUM problem (find a+b+c=0 in array) has conjectured complexity:
facts.truth: Ω(n²) in comparison model; recent progress shows n^(2-o(1)) is tight under some models
distractors: Ω(n log n) in comparison model; recent progress shows n^(2-o(1)) is tight under some models
O(n log n) in comparison model; recent progress shows n^(2-o(1)) is tight under some models
O(n) in comparison model; recent progress shows n^(2-o(1)) is tight under some models

facts.context: The Exponential Time Hypothesis (ETH) states that:
facts.truth: 3-SAT cannot be solved in 2^(o(n)) time (implies many conditional lower bounds)
distractors: 3-SAT cannot be solved in 3^(o(n)) time (implies many conditional lower bounds)
3-SAT can be solved in 2^(o(n)) time (implies no conditional lower bounds)
3-SAT cannot be solved in 2^(o(m)) time (implies many conditional lower bounds)

facts.context: The complexity of integer factoring is:
facts.truth: unknown; in NP ∩ coNP but not known to be in P or NP-complete; quantum polynomial via Shor
distractors: known polynomial; in P via the general number field sieve; no quantum speedup via Shor
proven NP-complete; not in coNP and not known to be in P; quantum exponential via Grover
unknown; in BPP ∩ coNP but not known to be in NP or NP-complete; quantum linear via Shor

facts.context: The log-rank conjecture in communication complexity states:
facts.truth: deterministic communication ≤ (log rank)^O(1) for any boolean matrix (open)
distractors: deterministic communication ≥ (log rank)^O(1) for any boolean matrix (open)
deterministic communication ≤ (log rank)^O(1) for any real matrix (open)
deterministic communication ≤ (log rank)^O(1) for any integer matrix (open)
